question: For the NYC high school that ranked in the top 7 by overall score in all three school years: 2006-07 and 2009-11, find the neighborhood named after this high school. Finally report the New York village where the builder of the earliest constructed bridge that connects this neighborhood with the mainland is buried. How many fewer people lived in that village at the 2020 census than in the village immediately to its south? Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact:
import os
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3

source = "datagov/2006-2007-school-progress-report/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
local_path = source if os.path.exists(source) else "/tmp/k123_data_cache/" + source

if os.path.exists(local_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(local_path, sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
else:
    obj = boto3.client("s3").get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
    df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
import re

school_col = "SCHOOL"
overall_col = [c for c in df.columns if "OVERALL SCORE" in c.upper()][0]
level_col_candidates = [c for c in df.columns if "SCHOOL LEVEL" in c.upper()]
level_col = level_col_candidates[0] if level_col_candidates else None

filtered = df.copy()
if level_col is not None:
    filtered = filtered[filtered[level_col].astype(str).str.contains("High", case=False, na=False)]

filtered[overall_col] = pd.to_numeric(filtered[overall_col], errors="coerce")
filtered = filtered.sort_values(overall_col, ascending=False)

def normalize_school(name):
    s = str(name)
    s = re.sub(r"\s+at\s+.+$", "", s, flags=re.I)
    s = re.sub(r"\bP\.?\s*S\.?\s*0*", "PS ", s, flags=re.I)
    s = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s).strip()
    if s.isupper():
        s = s.title()
        for small_word in ("and", "for", "of", "the"):
            s = re.sub(rf"\b{small_word.title()}\b", small_word, s)
    return s

answer = filtered[school_col].dropna().astype(str).head(7).map(normalize_school).tolist()
1.subquestion: Which high schools were in the top 7 by overall score in 2006-07?
answer: Marble Hill High School for International Studies

3.fact:
import os
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3

source = "datagov/2010-2011-school-progress-report/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
local_path = source if os.path.exists(source) else "/tmp/k123_data_cache/" + source

if os.path.exists(local_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(local_path, sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
else:
    obj = boto3.client("s3").get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
    df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), sep=",", engine="c", low_memory=False)
import re

school_col = "SCHOOL"
overall_col = [c for c in df.columns if "OVERALL SCORE" in c.upper()][0]
level_col = [c for c in df.columns if "SCHOOL LEVEL" in c.upper()][0]

filtered = df[df[level_col].astype(str).str.contains("High", case=False, na=False)].copy()
filtered = filtered[~filtered[school_col].astype(str).str.contains(r",\s*The\s*$", na=False, regex=True)]
filtered[overall_col] = pd.to_numeric(filtered[overall_col], errors="coerce")
filtered = filtered.sort_values(overall_col, ascending=False)

def normalize_school(name):
    s = str(name)
    s = re.sub(r"\s+at\s+.+$", "", s, flags=re.I)
    s = re.sub(r"\bP\.?\s*S\.?\s*0*", "PS ", s, flags=re.I)
    s = re.sub(r"\s+", " ", s).strip()
    return s

answer = filtered[school_col].dropna().astype(str).head(7).map(normalize_school).tolist()
3.subquestion: Which high schools were in the top 7 by overall score in 2010-11?
answer: Williamsburg High School for Architecture and Design
Marble Hill High School for International Studies

4.fact: In 1693 Frederick Philipse, a Frisian-born merchant who had sworn allegiance to the Crown upon the British takeover of Dutch New Netherlands in 1664, built the King's Bridge at Marble Hill near what is now West 230th Street in the Bronx.
4.subquestion: Who built the earliest constructed bridge that connects the neighborhood named after <intersection of node_1, node_2, node_3> with the mainland?

5.fact: Philipse died in 1702 and is buried with his two wives in the crypt of the Old Dutch Church of Sleepy Hollow.
5.subquestion: In which village is <node_4 answer> buried?

6.fact: The village is located on the east bank of the Hudson River, approximately  north of Midtown Manhattan in New York City, and is served by the Philipse Manor stop on the Metro-North Hudson Line. To the south of Sleepy Hollow is the village of Tarrytown, and to the north and east are unincorporated parts of Mount Pleasant. The population of the village at the 2020 census was 9,986.
6.subquestion: What was the population of <hop 3 answer> at the 2020 census?

7.fact: Tarrytown is a village in the town of Greenburgh in Westchester County, New York, United States, in the New York metropolitan area. It is located on the eastern bank of the Hudson River, approximately  north of Midtown Manhattan in New York City, and is served by a stop on the Metro-North Hudson Line. To the north of Tarrytown is the village of Sleepy Hollow (formerly "North Tarrytown"). The Tappan Zee Bridge crosses the Hudson at Tarrytown, carrying the New York State Thruway (Interstates 87 and 287) to South Nyack, Rockland County and points in Upstate New York. The population was 11,860 at the 2020 census.
7.subquestion: What was the 2020 census population of the village immediately south of <hop 3 answer>?
